name: Instapaper Read-Later Service API
what_it_does: Read-later service with REST API — enabling users to save web articles for offline reading with text extraction and formatting, highlighting and annotations, folder organization, and developer API for building reading list integrations, with clean text extraction (full-text parse) for readability-optimized display.
use_cases: Saving URLs to Instapaper reading list via API for bookmarklet and integration automation
Retrieving saved articles and highlights from Instapaper via API for personal knowledge management systems
Organizing Instapaper bookmarks into folders via API for content curation workflow
Extracting Instapaper highlights and annotations via API for note-taking system integration
Building read-later functionality into applications using Instapaper as backend storage
not_for: Team content sharing and collaboration (Instapaper is single-user focused)
Full text search across saved content (use Readwise Reader for search-heavy workflows)
AI-powered content analysis (use Readwise Reader for AI summary and tagging)
best_when: A developer building personal productivity tools needs a simple, battle-tested read-later API with clean text extraction — or a user wants to sync their Instapaper reading list with knowledge management tools like Notion or Obsidian.
avoid_when: You need team collaboration, AI summaries, advanced search, or PDF/video reading (Instapaper is web articles only).

auth.notes: OAuth 1.0a for user authentication. Bearer token access. User-scoped access (single-user reading list). No webhooks — polling required for list changes. REST API for bookmark and folder management. No OAuth2 (uses older OAuth 1.0a — gotcha for modern implementations). No fine-grained scopes. iOS and Android apps as primary interface. E-reader sync (Kindle, Kobo) via apps.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: basic features, no offline sync limit on mobile
pricing.paid_tiers: Premium $2.99/month or $29.99/year (full-text search, notes, text-to-speech, speed reading)
pricing.notes: New York company. Founded 2008 by Marco Arment. Acquired by Pinterest in 2016. Now operated as independent product under Pinterest. Simple, focused read-later service with strong text extraction. 5M+ users. Pioneer in read-later market. Significant competition from Pocket (Mozilla), Readwise Reader, and Matter. Pinterest ownership provides infrastructure stability but limited product development investment. Premium features relatively affordable. API has been largely stable since 2012.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; request reasonable use

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for bookmark management. OAuth 1.0a (older auth standard). Simple API with bookmark save, list, update, delete. No webhooks. Pinterest-owned with limited API investment. API stable but not actively expanded. Rate limits not documented. OAuth 1.0a requires HMAC-SHA1 signature computation — more complex than modern OAuth2.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Bookmark reads fully idempotent. Folder reads idempotent. Bookmark creation is idempotent for same URL (returns existing). Folder creation not idempotent — check by title.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth 1.0a (not OAuth2) — requires HMAC-SHA1 signature computation, more complex implementation
Pinterest-owned — limited API investment and development; API unchanged since ~2012
No webhooks — must poll for reading list changes
Rate limits not documented — implement conservative request throttling
US data residency only — EU customers should evaluate Wallabag (self-hosted) or Omnivore

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant (Pinterest EU obligations). US data residency. OAuth 1.0a auth. Pinterest-owned with corporate security standards. Reading list data includes user browsing habits — privacy-sensitive. OAuth 1.0a consumer secret must be kept secure. No scope granularity — full reading list access per OAuth grant. Pinterest parent company subject to US data broker regulations.
